title: Bengaluru creche abuse case: Caregiver arrested, more arrests likely
content: The case came to light after purported videos showing the alleged mistreatment of children at the creche, Little Buds Day Care, surfaced on social media

title: Govt lens on e-rickshaw remote shutdown issue; two apps removed from app stores
content: The issue drew public attention after reports surfaced that some e-rickshaws were being rendered inoperable through a remote shutdown feature linked to the Chinese smartphone application BAT-BMS

title: Supreme Court refuses to stay bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi in honeymoon murder case
content: While indicating that it had “reservations” about the reasoning adopted by the High Court, the top court said it was equally conscious of the implications of sending an accused back to custody after release on bail

title: ₹35-crore TVK MLA bribery case: Senthilbalaji’s brother Ashok Kumar moves Madras HC for anticipatory bail
content: Claims that he has no connection whatsoever with the case and that he had been roped into the matter with a political motive

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru
content: The seven deceased include five workers from M.P., and one each from Chhattisgarh and Karnataka; owners and supervisors of adjacent quarries charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 18
content: Currently, 14 women are undergoing treatment – one each on ventilator support and oxygen support, and 12 stable

title: Fishermen warned off Andhra Pradesh coast as heavy rain, strong winds forecast
content: The Cyclone Warning Centre has forecast heavy rain in Srikakulam, Vizianagaram and Alluri Sitharama Raju districts, and winds up to 65 kmph off the coast, over the next four days

title: Operation Numkhor: Customs may summon Dulquer Salmaan again for questioning
content: Among the 60-odd vehicles seized by Customs under the drive are three vehicles owned by Dulquer Salmaan and one which has another owner but which the agency suspects is retained by the actor

title: Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 17
content: Of the 83 persons who were affected in the ammonia gas leak that occurred on June 21, 15 women are still hospitalised — one on ventilator support.

title: BJP urges Karnataka Chief Minister to declare drought in affected taluks and take relief measures on war footing
content: Leader of Opposition in the Assembly R. Ashok has stated that drought situation had gripped about 150 taluks of the State following inadequate rainfall under the southwest monsoon

title: PM Modi to make first official visit to New Zealand next week
content: The two nations signed a free ​trade ‌agreement in April that will eliminate 95% of tariffs ‌on goods from ​New Zealand

title: Jupally challenges KTR and Harish to open debate on Telangana’s debts
content: Excise Minister said Telangana’s total outstanding liabilities increased from ₹90,161 crore at the time of State formation in June 2014 to ₹8,21,651 crore by December 1, 2023, when the BRS left office

title: Do not hide data on crime like previous regime: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari tells Police
content: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari inaugurated a dedicated help desk for women as well as a cybercrime help desk across all police stations in the State

title: U.P. pushes policy reforms, cluster-based development approach to transform the poultry ecosystem
content: The poultry sector in Uttar Pradesh has witnessed significant growth over the last decade, resulting in a substantial improvement in egg availability for consumers

title: Lokesh lays foundation for ₹6.30-crore works at Mangalagiri Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy temple
content: The works include a new Yagasala and the renovation of the Vahanasala, Ghanta Mandapam and Sri Krishna Devaraya Mandapam; the Minister asked officials to draw up a master plan for the Mangalagiri temple

title: Madras High Court denies advance bail to Anitha R. Radhakrishnan for his objectionable remarks against CM Vijay
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan asks how can a former Minister and a sitting MLA use such language against an elected Chief Minister

title: Places of Worship Act does not bar acquisition of religious sites for public purpose, says Allahabad HC
content: The observation was made by a Division Bench of Justice J.J. Munir and Justice Arun Kumar while dismissing a petition filed by six Muslim shopkeepers from Varanasi’s Dalmandi area challenging the proposed road-widening project linked to the Kashi Vishwanath Corridor

title: Cauvery realisation by Tamil Nadu in June back to the usual deficit pattern
content: The State realised a little less than three thousand million cubic feet, as against the stipulated 9.19 tmc ft for the month

title: AMMA crisis: Actor Shwetha Menon says ‘will continue’ as president till next election
content: In a social media post, she alleges that some 10 to 15 members had attended AMMA general body meeting on Thursday ‘with an agenda’ to oust her panel

title: ‘Agri’ Krishnamurthy challenges deserters to prove alleged bid to form government with DMK’s support
content: All the 47 legislators who had won on the ticket of the AIADMK had adopted a resolution, electing Palaniswami the leader of the legislature party, he says

title: One can’t be externed merely for taking part in agitations and protests against govt: Bombay HC
content: The order was passed on a plea filed by Saeed Ahmad Abdul Wahid Chaudhary, who belongs to the Social Democratic Party of India, challenging the action against him by the Mumbai police.

title: CPI(M) leader Puthalath Dinesan admits party’s lapses led to Kerala Assembly poll defeat
content: In a Facebook post, Puthalath Dinesan says ‘several shortcomings’ in party’s election campaign, including candidate selection, delay in taking action in Sabarimala gold loss row and failure to effectively counter UDF’s campaign, contributed to LDF’s defeat

title: Why Bengaluru is India’s deadliest metropolitan city for deaths arising from civic negligence
content: For six consecutive years, Bengaluru has recorded the highest number of deaths due to civic negligence among metropolitan cities. Has much changed on the ground with major administrative overhauls? Chetan B C explores.

title: Why is SpiceJet not operating from Chennai anymore? | Explained
content: The airline’s available fleet has shrunk to around 12-13 aircraft from more than 30 a few months ago, forcing the temporary suspension of services to several destinations, including Chennai, Guwahati and Varanasi.

title: Adani-MSC Vizhinjam port deal controversy | Explained
content: The move by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited to divest its 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Private Limited to Switzerland-based MSC Group has courted controversy, with Kerala govt conveying its displeasure to APSEZ on being kept in the dark

title: CPI(M) alleges trade union leader was arrested to stop protest before CM’s visit
content: Party State secretary V. Srinivasa Rao said the police detained Ch. Chandrasekhar to prevent protests against the Centre’s proposed employment scheme before the visit of the Chief Minister and a Union Minister

title: Radio telemetry almost saved this vulture
content: Radio-tagged and named Z25, this White-rumped vulture was saved many a time by critical data relayed by conservation scientists to forest personnel and NGO partners at Mudumalai Tiger Reserve. But before a life-saving decision could be implemented based on that data, Z25 ran into a power transmission line

title: Urdu is an Indian language, says Murli Manohar Joshi
content: Senior BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi hailed the efforts made during the NDA government led by former PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee, to encourage Indian Muslims to read the Quran in Urdu instead of Arabic

title: Two Kashmir Shia clerics ‘disallowed’ to travel to Iran for Ali Khamenei’s final rites
content: J&K Anjuman-e-Sharie Shian president Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosavi Al-Safavi was stopped by Bureau of Immigration; Shia cleric Masroor Abbas Ansari was also “denied a passport”

title: Maharashtra TET scam: Three more persons arrested from Punjab, Bihar
content: The names of the arrested accused are Kapil Dahiya, Mithunkumar Sublal Singh and Sonukumar Kushweshwar Singh. They have been remanded to police custody till July 9

title: CPI(M) sore over UDF government proposing four locations for AIIMS in Kerala
content: Party leaders say that the LDF government had already identified Kinalur in Kozhikode and land acquisition is in final stages

title: NIMHANS opens India’s first public sleep database for stroke patients
content: The database, which has been made publicly available, is expected to support research on sleep disorders in stroke survivors and help improve post-stroke care

title: What are e-OCI cards | Explained
content: OCI card is a lifelong visa for OCIs to enter and stay in India and exempts them from reporting to the police authorities

title: Salman Khan house firing case: Anmol Bishnoi seeks to surrender before court for ‘fair trial’
content: In the plea filed before a special MCOCA court, Anmol stated that he intended to voluntarily surrender before the court to “face the ongoing trial and join the judicial proceedings in the interest of fair trial and further in the interest of justice”.

title: Chandanappally church swaps fireworks for a home and hope
content: Parish heeds the Catholicos’ request to forego fireworks for the church festival in the wake of the Mundakkai fireworks tragedy and redirect the savings towards building homes for deserving families

title: West Bengal Cabinet approves panel to examine draft Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Minister Agnimitra Paul says the committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Prakash Desai will have four weeks to scrutinise the draft legislation and submit its recommendations

title: Gruha Jyothi verification raises concern over benefits in Karnataka
content: Consumers have been asked to provide Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN card, tenancy or rental agreement, caste certificate and ration card details during this verification process

title: Bhagyaraj, Bharathiraja and the memories they left behind
content: The two reshaped Tamil cinema with distinctive storytelling, memorable characters and authentic portrayals of human relationships

title: US warned Iran of alleged Israeli plot to kill negotiators; Araghchi, Ghalibaf among targets
content: US officials privately alerted Iran that Israel might target top negotiators, including Foreign Minister Araghchi and Speaker Ghalibaf, during peace talks. Washington feared such assassinations would derail diplomacy and reignite conflict, urging Israel against targeting Iran's political leadership. These warnings highlight growing US-Israel differences on Iran policy, with the US favoring negotiation while Israel advocates a tougher stance.

title: SC refuses to stay bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi in husband murder case
content: The Supreme Court has declined to halt bail for Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of orchestrating her husband's murder. Sonam is currently in Shillong as per bail terms. The Meghalaya government's plea to cancel her bail, citing a minor legal citation error as insufficient grounds, was rejected by the apex court. The trial court had previously granted bail due to procedural lapses in the arrest notification.

title: DMK MLA Anitha arrested over 'defamatory' remarks against TN CM Vijay
content: DMK leader and former minister Anitha R. Radhakrishnan was arrested today after the Madras High Court denied his anticipatory bail. The arrest follows alleged inflammatory comments made by Radhakrishnan targeting TVK president and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay at a public gathering. Police took him into custody following the court's decision, with investigations continuing.

title: Why equaliser was ruled out: FIFA explains VAR drama in Portugal vs Croatia match
content: FIFA confirmed that Croatia's dramatic 103rd-minute equaliser against Portugal was correctly ruled out after Connected Ball Technology detected a slight touch by Igor Matanovic before the ball reached offside player Mario Pasalic. The decision, backed by the match ball's IMU sensor data, denied Croatia a late equaliser as Portugal advanced with a 2-1 victory.

title: Centre removes 2 apps from app stores after e-rickshaw remote shutdown reports
content: Authorities have removed two smartphone applications, including the Chinese BAT-BMS app, from app stores following reports of e-rickshaws being remotely disabled. These apps, designed for battery management, were allegedly exploited to disrupt vehicles by connecting to unsecured Bluetooth-enabled battery systems. The government is urging app stores to enhance scrutiny to prevent the availability of such potentially harmful applications.

title: As SAP restricts hiring and travel, company stresses 'focus on AI'
content: Europe's software giant SAP is tightening its belt, implementing hiring freezes and halting non-essential travel to channel funds into artificial intelligence development. This strategic shift prioritizes core AI roles and acquisitions, aiming to secure the company's long-term competitiveness in an AI-driven industry. These measures follow a significant restructuring and reflect a broader industry trend of investing in AI while cutting costs.

title: 'When will Sooryavanshi be unleashed?': ABD disagrees with India coach's approach
content: Cricket circles are buzzing over teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's delayed India debut. While assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate advocates for a gradual integration, former Proteas captain AB de Villiers argues the Ireland series was the perfect platform. Legends like Shastri and Vaughan also voiced similar sentiments, suggesting Sooryavanshi should have been given a chance to gain international experience against a less formidable opponent.

title: After Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: 'Don't tell my wife': How Venezuelan man trapped under rubble for 8 days was rescued
content: A security guard, Hernan Alberto Gil Flores, miraculously survived eight days trapped under rubble after twin earthquakes in Venezuela. Rescuers, including international teams, painstakingly tunneled for over 100 hours, maintaining contact and providing sustenance. His survival, a beacon of hope amidst devastation, saw him emerge to a tearful reunion, offering immense relief to his family and a nation reeling from tragedy.

title: Yamal shatters Mbappe's record as Spain storm into FIFA World Cup Round of 16
content: Spain's Lamine Yamal has etched his name in history, becoming the youngest European player to achieve 10 major tournament victories at just 18 years old. He surpassed Kylian Mbappe's record by a significant margin during Spain's dominant 3-0 win over Austria. Despite an injury setback, Yamal showcased his electrifying form, earning Player of the Match. Spain's flawless defensive record continues as they advance to face Portugal.

title: ‎He failed 35 government exams before cracking UPSC and becoming an IAS officer
content: Vijay Vardhan's extraordinary journey saw him fail 35 government exams before achieving his dream. After years of relentless effort and heartbreak, he finally cracked the UPSC, first becoming an IPS officer in 2018 and then an IAS officer in 2021 with an impressive rank. His story is a testament to unparalleled perseverance, inspiring countless aspirants facing similar challenges in India's competitive exam landscape.

title: CA foundation May result 2026 toppers list: Sakshi Jain secures AIR 1 with 92.75%
content: ICAI has announced the CA Foundation May 2026 toppers along with the result declaration. Sakshi Jain from Nashik secured All India Rank 1 with 371 marks (92.75%). Ayanabbas Ajani and Radha Unmesh Mulay secured Rank 2 and 3 respectively. The merit list shows narrow score gaps among top performers. The examination was held across 609 centres in India and abroad, with 18,124 qualifiers out of 90,217 candidates, reflecting a 20.09% pass percentage overall declared ICAI.

title: Sharp rebound! India’s crude inventory hits almost 1-year high
content: According to estimates from Kpler, a global real-time data and analytics provider, India's crude oil inventory stood at 104 million barrels at the end of June, up significantly from 90.5 million barrels at the end of April. With the closure of the Strait of Hormuz during the conflict threatening nearly 40% of India's crude oil imports, refiners moved quickly to source alternative supplies from global markets.

title: Air India told to pay Rs 60,000 to elderly couple over pre-booked seat change
content: An elderly couple received Rs 60,000 compensation from Air India for a service deficiency. The airline wrongly changed their pre-booked seats, causing significant physical pain to the husband, who had hip surgery, and mental distress to his wife. This led to the husband being bedridden and missing a family function. The State Consumer Commission found the initial Rs 40,000 award insufficient for their suffering and litigation expenses.

title: Lesson from Operation Sindoor? Pakistan is raising a China-style rocket force
content: Following Operation Sindoor, Pakistan established the Army Rocket Force Command to bolster conventional strike capabilities, aiming to counter India's below-threshold responses. India's successful defense, utilizing S-400 systems, blunted Pakistani attacks during the conflict. This move highlights a shift towards non-contact warfare, with both nations re-evaluating their military strategies and procurement plans.

title: Is July 3 a federal holiday? What is open and closed on Friday ahead of Fourth of July
content: As the United States gears up for its 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, 2026, the preceding Friday, July 3, will be observed as a federal holiday. While most retail stores and postal services will operate normally, banks will be open with potential adjusted hours. The stock market, however, will be closed. State DMVs and city offices will see varied closures, so checking local schedules is advised.

title: Watch: Croatia fans throw bottles after VAR denies last-minute equaliser
content: Croatia's World Cup campaign ended in heartbreak after a 103rd-minute equaliser by Josko Gvardiol was ruled out by VAR for offside against Portugal. The decision sparked protests from Croatian fans and players. After Ivan Perisic's opener and Cristiano Ronaldo's penalty equaliser, Goncalo Ramos scored the winner, sending Portugal into the last 16.

title: Why endurance sport is no longer a niche pursuit in India
content: India's endurance movement is steadily growing beyond a niche community of runners and cyclists. Record participation at road races, the rise of run clubs and a younger generation embracing marathons, triathlons and ultra-runs reflect a changing fitness culture. IRONMAN triathlete Renee Noronha and ultra-runner Sufiya Sufi are among the athletes witnessing that change, while speaking about the preparation, patience, and consistency that endurance sports demand.

title: Salmonella cases tied to noodles rise to 106 across Europe: Here's what happened
content: A Salmonella outbreak linked to flavored instant noodles has affected 106 people across 13 EU countries and the UK, with nearly half hospitalized. Health officials suspect a single Ukrainian producer, identifying the Salmonella Stanley strain. Investigations point to contaminated chicken and hot chicken flavored noodles distributed through Poland. Recalls are underway, but officials warn of continued risk due to long shelf life.

title: Bengaluru daycare horror raises a larger question: Who watches the caregivers?
content: Shocking videos of alleged toddler abuse in a Bengaluru daycare have sparked national outrage, questioning India's childcare systems. As dual-income families rise, workplace daycares are essential, yet safeguards lag behind rapid growth. The incident highlights the vulnerability of young children who cannot articulate mistreatment, and the urgent need for stricter regulation, caregiver training, and corporate accountability beyond just providing space. This demands a robust child protection framework.

title: Nagpur road rage: Bikers drag cop out of car, assault him, smash his vehicle
content: A traffic cop faced a brutal assault and vehicle damage from eight bikers on Mankapur Road after a minor traffic dispute. The mob, exhibiting aggressive behavior and issuing death threats, vandalized his car before fleeing. Prompt police action, aided by CCTV footage and witness accounts, led to the arrest of fourteen individuals, including three with prior criminal records.

title: Ketan Agarwal murder case: Siya Goyal shows middle finger to media while being escorted by police
content: Accused Siya Goyal, facing charges in her fiancé's murder, allegedly displayed defiance during a police investigation at her residence where crucial evidence was recovered. Authorities also took her to a site where she and a co-accused reportedly rehearsed the crime. Police are seeking court permission for polygraph tests on both accused, aiming to unravel the motive behind the realtor's death at Lohagad Fort.

title: Explained: Why Croatia's 103rd-minute equaliser against Portugal was ruled out
content: On Thursday, Croatia's stoppage-time equaliser against Portugal was ruled out after VAR determined that Igor Matanovic had made a slight touch before the ball reached offside player Mario Pasalic, who assisted Josko Gvardiol's goal. Officials used ball sensor "Snicko" technology to confirm the touch, sparking outrage among Croatian players and fans.

title: FIFA WC: How Round of 16 is shaping up as Portugal set up blockbuster Spain showdown
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 enters its thrilling Round of 16, with Portugal's dramatic 2-1 win over Croatia, fueled by a Cristiano Ronaldo penalty, setting up a highly anticipated clash with rivals Spain. As the knockout bracket solidifies, other confirmed ties include Canada vs Morocco and Brazil vs Norway, with England facing Mexico. The stakes are higher than ever as teams vie for a spot in the quarterfinals.

title: ICAI CA foundation result May 2026 declared; overall pass percentage stands at 20.09%
content: ICAI has declared the CA Foundation Examination May 2026 results, with an overall pass percentage of 20.09%. Out of 90,217 candidates who appeared, 18,124 qualified. Male pass rate stood at 20.88% and female at 19.24%. Sakshi Jain topped the exam with 371 marks, followed by Ayanabbas Ajani and Radha Unmesh Mulay. Results are available on the ICAI portal, and the ICAI President congratulated successful candidates. The examination was conducted across 609 centres in India abroad.

title: FIFA World Cup: Switzerland end Algeria's dream to storm into Round of 16
content: Switzerland secured their spot in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a commanding 2-0 win over Algeria, thanks to goals from Breel Embolo and Dan Ndoye. This victory marks their fourth consecutive appearance in the knockout stages, and they now aim for a deeper run, having not won a last 16 match since 1938. Youngster Johan Manzambi has been a standout performer, driving their success.

title: Sailor's body returned from Venezuela with 'missing' organs; Indian embassy seeks probe
content: The Indian Embassy in Venezuela is demanding a full investigation into allegations that Indian sailor Rakesh Chauhan's organs were removed before his body was sent home. His family claims he died suspiciously on a Venezuelan vessel, and a second post-mortem in India revealed his body was completely devoid of internal organs. The Forward Seamen's Union of India is also pushing for answers and has highlighted discrepancies in repatriation documents.

title: 'We did something nobody knew': Trump details secret US operations near Hormuz
content: President Trump revealed US forces have repeatedly destroyed Iran's radar systems, forcing Tehran to rebuild them multiple times. He detailed stealth operations along the coast and strikes in response to attacks on shipping. Meanwhile, indirect talks in Doha concluded without a major breakthrough, focusing on maritime traffic and unfreezing funds, with nuclear program discussions expected later.

title: Heavy rain batters Mumbai, IMD issues orange alert; high-tide expected
content: Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and Raigad are under an orange alert as the IMD predicts moderate to intense rain spells. Heavy monsoon showers have already drenched the city, with the island city recording 114 mm of rainfall in 24 hours. Suburbs also experienced significant downpours, prompting authorities to advise residents to take necessary precautions.

title: Born 50 yrs after Kasparov! How Pratitee became India's lone medallist at World Youth Chess
content: Born exactly 50 years after Garry Kasparov, 13-year-old chess prodigy and maths Olympiad star Pratitee Bordoloi scripted history by winning India's only medal at the 2026 FIDE World Youth Chess Championship. The story explores her extraordinary rise, analytical brilliance, disciplined upbringing, and the unique blend of academic excellence, humility, and competitive ambition shaping her journey.

title: Asics Rs 6,499 running shoes lost sole in a month; panel orders compensation
content: A Chandigarh consumer commission has ordered Asics India to refund Rs 6,499 for a pair of defective running shoes, along with 6% annual interest and Rs 10,000 in compensation. The commission found the company guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice after the shoes' sole separated within a month of purchase.

title: PM Modi lists 'five sankalps' for Amarnath Yatra pilgrims as second batch leaves Jammu
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has extended his best wishes for the annual Shri Amarnath Yatra, urging pilgrims to embrace five key resolutions focusing on cleanliness, safety, environmental care, supporting local economies, and national service. The second batch of devotees has commenced their journey under robust security measures, with enhanced technology-driven surveillance in place. The pilgrimage, a significant symbol of India's spiritual heritage, continues until August 28.

title: 'Our dream': Vozinha's rallying cry ahead of Cabo Verde's showdown with Argentina
content: Cabo Verde's veteran goalkeeper Vozinha has issued an emotional plea for national unity ahead of their historic FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against Argentina. The smallest nation to ever reach the knockout stages, the Blue Sharks, are preparing to face the defending champions. Vozinha emphasised that "tomorrow we are one. One people, one flag, one heart beating for Cape Verde," inspiring his nation to dream big.

title: 'Rs 2 lakh salary, no female friends, 24x7 live location': Woman's husband checklist viral
content: A woman's extensive list of demands for a future husband, including virginity, a high salary, and constant location sharing, has ignited a fierce online debate. While many criticized her expectations as unrealistic, others defended her right to set standards. The viral post prompted a range of reactions, from humorous quips about ordering a custom AI husband to sharp critiques of perceived hypocrisy in modern dating.

title: Fuel-hit Russia bought gasoline produced by India’s Nayara via traders: Report
content: Russia is reportedly sourcing gasoline from India via international traders to counter fuel shortages stemming from Ukrainian attacks on its infrastructure. Nayara Energy, with a significant stake held by Russian oil giant Rosneft, is identified as a key supplier. While Indian officials state no direct sales, they acknowledge indirect sourcing through trading firms. This development follows reports of substantial gasoline shipments from India to Russia.

title: 'I wish this was AI': Parents outraged over abuse of toddlers at Bengaluru daycare
content: Shocking allegations of toddler abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, operating on Capgemini's campus, have ignited outrage among working parents. Demands for livestreamed activities and constant surveillance are surging. The incident highlights a critical lack of oversight, leaving vulnerable children at risk and shattering parental trust in essential childcare support systems. Companies are now urged to implement stringent safety measures and robust governance for workplace daycares.

title: Ayodhya trust not answerable to government, MHA told CIC last year
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, managing the Ayodhya temple, operates independently, with the Home Ministry confirming it's not accountable to the government. This autonomy is crucial as the trust faces a July 6 meeting to address resignations amid a donation row. Decisions on key figures like Champat Rai and Anil Mishra will be made internally by its members, highlighting the trust's self-governing structure.

title: Who is the quickest at WC? This Australian tops speed charts ahead of Mbappe, Haaland
content: Australia's Jordan Bos is a breakout star at the FIFA World Cup, showcasing blistering speed that outpaces Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland. The 23-year-old full-back, now a key player for the Socceroos, faces Egypt in a historic knockout clash. Bos's rapid ascent from Australia's domestic league to European giants highlights his impressive talent as the nation chases a World Cup breakthrough.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: 49ers, Cowboys linked to Browns' $4.65M QB
content: The Cleveland Browns’ star player Shedeur Sanders has been a popular name concerning trade rumors ahead of the new season, sparking concern about his future. Amid all the teams that have been linked to him, it seems the Dallas Cowboys and the San Francisco 49ers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Hyderabad Woman Checks Into Hotel, Dies By Suicide On Video Call With Friend
content: According to investigators, Renuka had checked into the hotel in Hyderabad's Langar Houz area with a man at around 3 am on Wednesday.

title: Bail Relief For Sonam Raghuvanshi To Continue After Confusion In Supreme Court
content: Sonam Raghuvanshi was arrested in June last year in connection with the murder of her husband, Raja Raghuvanshi.

title: Provident Fund Contribution Cap At Rs 1,800: What It Means For Your Salary
content: EPFO New Rule 2026: The mandatory contribution will now apply only on the statutory wage ceiling of Rs 15,000 a month.

title: Pune Toddler Dies After Falling Into Rainwater-Filled Sewage Pit
content: The victim, Soham Lakhan Kasbe, was playing in front of his house when he fell into one of the unguarded pits.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 500 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices held steady at $72 the tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased.

title: Pachpadra Refinery Set To Boost Rajasthan Economy, PM To Inaugurate Tomorrow
content: The refinery is being projected as a key step towards making India self-reliant, while also reinforcing the political message of development.

title: El Nino Set To Intensify Between July And September, Warns UN
content: El Nino conditions have set in and are forecast to rapidly strengthen into a strong event by September, raising risks of heatwaves worldwide

title: Opinion: Opinion | What's Behind Pakistan's Growing Indus Treaty 'Threats' To India? Its Own Helplessness
content: Pakistan's hysterics and fits haven't impressed India, much less make her re-consider her stand on the treaty. If Islamabad is trying to threaten its way to water, it is already failing.

title: J&K Government Issues Strict Advisory As Amarnath Yatra Begins
content: Alongside the start of the yatra, Jammu and Kashmir authorities issued a fresh advisory, making it clear that pilgrims should not travel to the Union Territory without advance registration.

title: The Dual Crisis Of Monsoon Deficit And Catastrophic Rain Across India
content: Around 79% of the geographical area of the country, spanning 29 states, recorded a status of 'Deficit' or 'Large Deficit' between June 1 and July 1.

title: 2 Caregivers Arrested For Abusing Children At Bengaluru Daycare
content: Horrifying videos of the abuse were shared by a complainant this week, showing children as young as two years old sitting inside the drum of a front-loading washing machine.

title: Pakistan Uses Cement On Vedic-Era Taxila Walls, Gets 'Delisting' Warning From UN
content: Taxila, located in the Rawalpindi district of Pakistan's Punjab province, is a vast serial site that includes a Mesolithic cave and the archaeological remains of four early settlement sites, Buddhist monasteries, and a mosque and madrassa.

title: Chennai Police Arrest 5 More In Rs 35-Crore Bribery Probe Involving TVK MLA
content: The case stems from a complaint by TVK MLA Dr N Ilayaraja, who alleged that pollster Thirunavukkarasu contacted him claiming to be acting on behalf of functionaries of a major political party.

title: WhatsApp, UPI, Speed Post: How Ganja Doorstep Delivery Worked And Busted
content: Investigators found that the gang received orders through WhatsApp and other social media platforms, accepted payments through multiple UPI accounts and dispatched eight to 10 Speed Post parcels daily.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Will Rs 18,000 Basic Pay Rise To Rs 69,000? What We Know
content: 8th Pay Commission: The Rs 69,000 figure has come from a particular demand made by employee unions. It is not an official proposal of the commission.

title: Israel Wanted To Kill Top Iranian Negotiators. How US Saved Them
content: Araghchi and Ghalibaf have been the key officials negotiating with various countries in the region to reach a framework agreement with the US that sought to open the Strait of Hormuz and set the outline for follow-on talks on Tehran's nuclear program.

title: "Ridiculous": Trump Criticises "One-Sided Relationship" With NATO
content: Donald Trump hit out at European allies over defense spending and the war in Iran as Washington moves to scale back its long-term NATO commitments.

title: No need to doubt transparency of SIR in Jharkhand, says CEO
content: K. Ravi Kumar says Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls is to ensure constitutional rights to Indian citizens;  foreign nationals living legally or illegally cannot participate and giving a false declaration is a criminal offence

title: The rise of Mahjong in Visakhapatnam
content: Once confined to niche circles, Mahjong, the centuries-old tile game is drawing a growing community of players in Visakhapatnam

title: In Visakhapatnam Central Jail, learning skills for life away from crime
content: The first-of-its-kind digital programme in Andhra Pradesh jails is steering remand prisoners toward meaningful employment after serving prison time. Primarily targeting individuals booked under the NDPS Act, the initiative focuses on rehabilitation and future financial independence of the inmates, explores Harish Gilai

title: Boulder collapse near Bengaluru: Half-eaten meals remain where a routine workday at quarry turned fatal
content: Most of the workers employed at the quarry are migrant labourers from Yadgir and Raichur in Karnataka, and from Jharkhand, Bihar and Madhya Pradesh

title: Mapping cancer, testing capacity
content: Telangana government’s decision to make cancer a notifiable disease promises to reveal its true burden in the State. But unless treatment infrastructure expands beyond Hyderabad, identifying more patients may only expose a healthcare system already stretched to its limits, writes Siddharth Kumar Singh

title: India, New Zealand ink “historic” free trade agreement, to be implemented later this year
content: The FTA, negotiations for which were announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025, is one of the fastest to have been negotiated by India

title: Madhya Pradesh judge faces mob fury as suo motu case to protect judicial officers sits mute in Supreme Court
content: Supreme Court Advocates on Record Association slams recent threats, targeted social media campaign against MP judge; 2021 case to safeguard judges, courts was last posted for hearing in March 2025

title: Bombay High Court directs State to secure former Judge’s safety
content: The Bench said both Justice Patel, his wife and also daughter, who are in Mumbai, should be adequately protected

title: Assam labourer shows 15 documents but fails citizenship test in High Court
content: The documents Aminul Hoque, a daily-wage labourer, submitted included a 1973 land purchase deed, a school certificate, and a copy of the 1951 National Register of Citizens

title: If a passport is not proof of citizenship, then what is? | Explained
content: The MEA in a statement said that a passport is a travel document, not a citizenship document. What does the law actually say?

title: After WhatsApp, Telegram and Signal receive notices on username feature
content: The notices follow directions to WhatsApp to pause its username feature over scam and impersonation concerns; Spokespersons for Telegram, Signal did not have an immediate comment to share

title: Watch: Russian missile tears through Ukraine swimming pool roof as people swim inside

title: Japanese PM tries her hand at santoor during India visit, earns applause: Watch

title: EC seeks TMC factions’ reply on split; bypolls may lead to symbol freeze
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More
